meta.total_giving_source: MacKenzie Scott cumulative giving since 2019, ~$26.3B to >1,600 orgs as of Dec 2025 ($7.17B in 2025 alone). CNBC 2025-12-13; Alliance Magazine 2025.

qaly_per_death_averted.source: Remaining life expectancy ~26y for low-income adults ~age 55 (the populations in Miller et al. 2021 and Bailey & Goodman-Bacon 2015); utility weight ~0.78 typical for this age/health. Discounted at 3%.

vsly_usd.source: HHS ASPE Standard Values for Regulatory Analysis 2026: VSLY central $611k (3% discount), $1.03M (7%); 2024 low estimate $231k. NOTE the high end reflects 7% discounting while QALYs here use 3%, so the monetization spans discount conventions and is approximate (affects only the $-value / benefit-cost ratio, never the QALY count).

frontier_cost_per_qaly_usd.source: GiveWell long-lasting insecticide-treated nets ~$78 per DALY averted ($3,340 per life saved); top-charity range ~$50-150 per QALY/DALY.

realization_factor.source: Implementation/attribution only (evidence quality now lives in causal_credibility). Center for Effective Philanthropy (2023): large unrestricted gifts materially expand recipient capacity/reach.

strong_quasi.design: Credible quasi-experiment (DD/IV) with admin data and checks

moderate_quasi.design: Quasi-experiment with notable concerns or a contested literature

projection.design: Model/IAM projection; no direct causal estimate for this lever

assumption.design: No causal health evidence; skeptical expert prior

health_coverage.label: Health - insurance & access
health_coverage.identification: DD across staggered state Medicaid expansions with linked administrative mortality (Miller et al. 2021). Strong; Oregon HIE RCT (null on 2-yr physical health) tempers it, hence not the top tier.            # cost_per_qaly = cost_per_life / qaly_per_death
health_coverage.source: Miller, Johnson & Wherry (2021), QJE 136(3): Medicaid expansion saved ~1 life/yr per 239-316 adults gaining coverage; implied $327k-$867k per life saved (0.132 pp annual mortality decline, 9.4% reduction).

health_chc.label: Health - community health centers
health_chc.identification: Event-study/DD on CHC rollout (Bailey & Goodman-Bacon 2015). Credible but 1960s-70s rollout with documented spatial-spillover concerns in later re-analyses; external validity to today's CHCs uncertain.  # = medicare_cost_per_lifeyear * chc_fraction
health_chc.source: Bailey & Goodman-Bacon (2015), AER 105(3): CHCs cut mortality among 50+ by ~2% within a decade, achieving the reduction at 1/8 to 1/3 the cost of the equivalent reduction through Medicare.

health_mental.label: Health - mental & behavioral
health_mental.identification: ICERs come from RCTs of collaborative care for depression (high internal validity). Discount here is mostly attribution (does Scott's grant fund this exact model), handled by realization, not identification.
health_mental.source: Collaborative-care depression systematic reviews (e.g., Jacob et al.; community guide): ICERs ~$17k-$49.5k/QALY (healthcare perspective), adolescents $18,239/QALY; program cost $104-$639/person/yr.

econ_cash.label: Economic security - cash & financial
econ_cash.identification: Lottery randomization (Cesarini et al. 2016) credibly estimates a ~null adult-mortality effect of income. High credibility for a SMALL effect: we trust the estimate, and the estimate is that there is little here.
econ_cash.source: Cesarini et al. (2016, QJE) lottery study finds ~zero causal effect of income on adult mortality in a rich country; EITC health effects exist but are contested/confounded. Most value here is non-health consumption and wellbeing, which QALYs do not capture -> high $/QALY, wide.

econ_food.label: Economic security - food security
econ_food.identification: SNAP/health links are largely associational with endogenous take-up (sicker or poorer households select in/out); strong confounding risk. The cost-per-QALY assumes the effect is real; credibility says it may not be causal.
econ_food.source: Cost-effectiveness range CONDITIONAL on the association being causal, anchored to (not derived from) CBPP syntheses and food-is-medicine RCTs: SNAP linked to ~$1,400/yr lower health costs and ~4% fewer hospitalizations (46% among food-insecure seniors). Causal doubt is carried by the observational credibility tier, NOT by inflating this range (avoids double-counting).

econ_housing.label: Economic security - housing & homelessness
econ_housing.identification: NASEM (2018) judged the PSH health evidence MIXED: few RCTs, mostly pre/post and quasi-experimental, contested cost-savings. Real signal for high-need subgroups, weak identification overall.
econ_housing.source: NASEM (2018) Permanent Supportive Housing review. The ~$62,493/QALY point is the Housing & Health Study, an HIV-positive unstably-housed cohort that NASEM reviews (not general chronic homelessness); PSH frequently cost-saving ($6,875-$33,502/person/yr) for high-need groups. Range conditional on a real effect; identification doubt is in the credibility tier.

econ_workforce.label: Economic security - workforce & mobility
econ_workforce.identification: Health effect inferred from the income-health gradient with a causal discount; little direct experimental evidence that workforce grants move health. Consistent with the credible ~null income-mortality result.
econ_workforce.source: Cost-effectiveness CONDITIONAL on workforce/earnings gains translating to health via the income-health gradient. The causal doubt (~null per Cesarini et al. 2016) is carried by the observational credibility tier, not this range. Most value is economic, not health -> high $/QALY, wide.

education.label: Education (HBCUs, comm. college, scholarships)
education.identification: Compulsory-schooling IV/quasi-experiments (Lleras-Muney; Lager & Torssander; Dutch reform) are credible but the literature is genuinely contested — Clark & Royer (2013) find ~null mortality effects. Wide.
education.source: van Kippersluis, O'Donnell & van Doorslaer (2011, Journal of Human Resources 46(4), Dutch reform): +1 yr schooling -> ~6% lower mortality; Lleras-Muney (2005); Card returns to schooling. Long lag; large non-health (earnings) value excluded.

equity_justice.label: Equity & justice
equity_justice.identification: No credible causal estimate links Scott's equity/justice grants to QALYs. Plausible indirect pathways (e.g., incarceration-mortality) exist but attribution is speculative; value is overwhelmingly non-health.
equity_justice.source: Racial equity, criminal-justice reform, immigration, gender. Health pathway is weak/indirect (e.g., reduced incarceration); most value is non-health (rights, fairness, opportunity). Very wide.

civic_democracy.label: Civic / democracy / philanthropy infra
civic_democracy.identification: No causal health evidence; value is civic/institutional.
civic_democracy.source: Negligible direct health pathway; value is civic and institutional.

arts_culture.label: Arts & culture
arts_culture.identification: Arts-and-health literature exists (WHO 2019; Bygren et al.) but is observational and about participation programs, not institutional grants. Treated as a skeptical near-zero prior.
arts_culture.source: Negligible direct health pathway; wellbeing value not captured by QALYs.

environment.label: Environment / climate
environment.identification: Mortality cost of carbon is an integrated-assessment-model projection (Bressler 2021), not a causal estimate of a gift's effect; abatement attribution of diffuse climate philanthropy is speculative.
environment.source: Wide $/QALY range ANCHORED TO, not derived from, Bressler (2021, Nature Communications) mortality cost of carbon (~4,434 tCO2 per excess death, 2020-2100): no abatement-leverage chain is modeled, so the effective $/QALY is a deliberately wide prior, not a calculation. Speculative.

other_community.label: Other / general community
other_community.identification: Blended social-services proxy; no single identified estimate.
other_community.source: Blended social-services proxy for unclassified giving.
